zh.Vieux-Moulin, Compiègne, Oise, Hauts-de-: 法國豪-德-福朗斯奧易維額-姆藍
zh.Bruyères-le-Châtel, Palaiseau, Essonne,: 法國i萊-德-福朗斯艾頌布盧艾雷勒-a特爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes du Moulin於大約1155年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯奧易維額-姆藍時，她的父親Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais32歲，母親Agnes de Soisy26歲。她和Pons de Bruyères le Châtel至少有1個女兒。她去世在法國i萊-德-福朗斯艾頌布盧艾雷勒-a特爾。

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Moulin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gaston, Michel, Pierre, Andre, Cecile, Chantal, Gabrielle, Herve, Jacques.

French and Walloon: from Old French molin ‘mill’ (from Latin molina), applied as a topographic name for someone who lived at a mill, or, by extension, as a metonymic occupational name for a miller or mill worker.

French and Walloon: habitational name from (Le) Moulin, the name of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia), named with Old French molin ‘mill’. Compare Demoulin and Dumoulin 1.

variants.name: Demoulin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Chantel, Honorine, Yves.

French and Walloon: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone who lived at a mill, French moulin, or a habitational name for someone from any of numerous places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named with this word. Compare Moulin 1 and 2, and also Demulling .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes du Moulin was born about 1155, in Vieux-Moulin, Oise, Hauts-de-France, France, her father, Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais, was 32 and her mother, Agnes de Soisy, was 26. She had at least 1 daughter with Pons de Bruyères le Châtel. She died in Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes du Moulin est née environ 1155, à Vieux-Moulin, Oise, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Soisy, avait 26 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Pons de Bruyères le Châtel. Elle est décédée à Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes du Moulin um 1155 in Vieux-Moulin, Oise, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais 32 und ihre Mutter Agnes de Soisy 26. Sie hatte mit Pons de Bruyères le Châtel mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb in Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes du Moulin è nata il circa 1155 nel Vieux-Moulin, Oise, Hauts-de-France, Francia, sua padre, Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Agnes de Soisy, aveva 26 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Pons de Bruyères le Châtel. Morì nel Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes du Moulinが約1155年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスオイゼヴィエウクス-モウリンで生まれた時，父Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinaisは32歳，母Agnes de Soisyは26歳であった。彼女はPons de Bruyères le Châtelとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女はフランスiレ-デ-フランスエソンブルイeレス-レ-チaテルで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes du Moulin(이)가 대략 1155년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 이스 비우스-모우린에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes de Soisy(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 Pons de Bruyères le Châtel(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 에손 브루이e레스-레-cha텔에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes du Moulin nasceu aproximadamente 1155, em Vieux-Moulin, Oise, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Soisy, tinha 26 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Pons de Bruyères le Châtel. Ela faleceu em Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes du Moulin родилась около 1155 г. в местности виеукс-моулинь, оис, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Soisy, 26. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Pons de Bruyères le Châtel. Она умерла в местности бруиeрес-ле-чaтел, эссонн, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes du Moulin nació aproximadamente 1155, en Vieux-Moulin, Oise, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Seigneur Adam I de Beaumont du Gatinais, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Agnes de Soisy, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Pons de Bruyères le Châtel. Murió en Bruyères-le-Châtel, Essonne, Île-de-France, Francia.
